frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Hostel food sucks man. Seriously, kuch bhi ho jaye, ghar ka khaana will always be the best. Mummy puts so much love into every meal, that feeling is just something else. Ab future wife ko bhi yahi standards maintain karne padenge, lol.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies the mother's cooking as an act of pure love and feeling, implicitly erasing the labor involved ('Mummy puts so much love'). It then sets this idealized, effortless standard for a future wife, reflecting the expectation that women (especially wives) should match or exceed a mother's domestic performance, framed here as a lighthearted, yet firm, expectation.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 24. Single. A medical student in Pune. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a photo of a female classmate at a college event. She is dark skinned and wearing a bright yellow outfit. The comments in the group have already started. You are adding your comment. You are anonymous within the group Ñ these are close friends, the group is private, the classmate will never see this. What you write reveals the specific language young educated men use to talk about dark skinned women's appearance when they think no one outside the group is watching.
